scaled_oracle_cases.description: Parse a THetA file with comments, blank lines, reordered columns, and extra whitespace.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: The function must skip blank lines and comment lines, use header to map columns regardless of order, and strip whitespace from values.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: File contains blank lines and # comments before header; columns are reordered; values have extra spaces. Parse correctly.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: Old behavior would fail to skip comments, assume column order, or crash on whitespace.
scaled_oracle_cases.setup_code:
sample = (
    "# This is a comment\n"
    "\n"
    "p*\tNLL\tC\tmu\n"
    " 0.1 \t 12.5 \t 2 \t 0.4 , 0.6\n"
)
fname = "theta_test.txt"
with open(fname, "w") as f:
    f.write(sample)
covered_requirements: Make the input file potentially contain blank lines and comment lines (starting with #) that must be skipped before parsing the header and data line.
Make the function use the header line to map column names to indices instead of assuming fixed column order.
Make the function robust to extra whitespace around values (e.g., spaces after commas or around numbers).

scaled_oracle_cases.description: Parse a file with an empty tumor fraction field, expecting None.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: The function must convert empty string fields to None.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: If a value in mu, C, or p* list is empty after stripping, set to None.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: Old behavior would raise ValueError (float('')) or produce wrong type.
scaled_oracle_cases.setup_code:
sample = (
    "NLL\tmu\tC\tp*\n"
    "12.5\t0.4,\t2\t0.1\n"
)
fname = "theta_test.txt"
with open(fname, "w") as f:
    f.write(sample)
covered_requirements: Make the function handle empty string fields within the comma-separated or colon-separated lists by converting them to None.

scaled_oracle_cases.description: File with mismatched number of copy number entries raises ValueError.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Raise ValueError if number of copy number entries does not match number of tumor fractions.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: Old behavior would parse inconsistent data without error.
scaled_oracle_cases.setup_code:
sample = (
    "NLL\tmu\tC\tp*\n"
    "12.5\t0.4,0.6,0.8\t2\t0.1,0.2\n"
)
fname = "theta_test.txt"
with open(fname, "w") as f:
    f.write(sample)
covered_requirements: Add validation that the number of tumor fractions matches the number of copy number entries and the number of probabilities, raising ValueError on mismatch.

scaled_oracle_cases.description: File with mismatched number of probabilities raises ValueError.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Raise ValueError if number of probabilities does not match number of tumor fractions.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: Old behavior would parse inconsistent data without error.
scaled_oracle_cases.setup_code:
sample = (
    "NLL\tmu\tC\tp*\n"
    "12.5\t0.4,0.6,0.8\t2:3\t0.1,0.2,0.3\n"
)
fname = "theta_test.txt"
with open(fname, "w") as f:
    f.write(sample)
covered_requirements: Add validation that the number of tumor fractions matches the number of copy number entries and the number of probabilities, raising ValueError on mismatch.

scaled_oracle_cases.description: File with missing required column raises ValueError.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Raise ValueError if a required column is missing from the header.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: If any required column name is not found in the header, raise ValueError.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: Old behavior might produce errors or gracefully handle but not required.
scaled_oracle_cases.setup_code:
sample = (
    "NLL\tmu\tp*\n"
    "12.5\t0.4,0.6\t0.1,0.2\n"
)
fname = "theta_test.txt"
with open(fname, "w") as f:
    f.write(sample)
covered_requirements: Make the function use the header line to map column names to indices instead of assuming fixed column order.
